headline: BREAKING: Iran fires missiles and drones at US military bases in Jordan, Bahrain & Kuwait — direct exchange escalates. CPI 4.2% in-line.
sub: Iran's Revolutionary Guard struck US military installations in Jordan, Bahrain, and Kuwait on Jun 10 in retaliation for US strikes near the Strait of Hormuz — the first time Iran has directly targeted US bases across multiple allied nations in this conflict. 'Just about all' incoming projectiles were intercepted per US officials. Trump threatened to attack Iran 'very hard' absent a peace deal; Qatari mediators flew to Tehran. May CPI landed at 4.2% YoY (in-line, core monthly 0.2% — softer than expected) — not a disaster but still near two-year highs and now complicated by fresh oil shock risk. The Strait of Hormuz situation has escalated materially and the June 17 Warsh debut presser is a policy knife-edge.

events.text: BREAKING — Iran Strikes US Bases: Jordan, Bahrain & Kuwait (Jun 10)
events.detail: Iran's Revolutionary Guard launched missiles and drones at US military bases in Jordan, Bahrain, and Kuwait on Jun 10 in direct retaliation for US strikes near the Strait of Hormuz. 'Just about all' projectiles intercepted. Trump threatened to hit Iran 'very hard' if no peace deal. Qatari mediators flew to Tehran. This is the first direct Iranian attack on US military installations across multiple allied nations — a major escalation beyond the Jun 9 exchange.

events.text: BREAKING — US Strikes Iran (Strait of Hormuz, Jun 9)
events.detail: US launched retaliatory strikes Jun 9 after Iran downed a US Apache helicopter off Oman. Explosions at Bandar Abbas and Qeshm — directly on the Strait of Hormuz. Ceasefire has collapsed. Crude oil spike and shipping disruption risk are acute. Nasdaq -3.5% intraday.

Ceasefire broken — US strikes hit ports flanking the strait. Shipping at just 5% of pre-war levels; further disruption could fully close the chokepoint and send crude to $120+. Iranian retaliation on Jun 10 adds further escalation risk.

events.text: May CPI (Wed Jun 10) — RESULT: 4.2% YoY, core +0.2% MoM
events.detail: 4.2% YoY came in-line with expectations; core monthly 0.2% softer than feared. Not a disaster but still near two-year highs. War premium in crude remains the dominant upside risk heading into June 17 Fed decision. Softer core may give Warsh some room but fresh Iran escalation today complicates the picture.
Producer prices complete the inflation picture ahead of Warsh's June 17 debut presser — energy shock from new strikes will show in the pipeline.
First major central bank move since Iran ceasefire; guidance sets EUR/USD tone and signals global policy direction — now complicated by fresh escalation with Iran striking US bases.
Post-payrolls repricing — watch 10-year yield for further pressure on rate-sensitive growth stocks. New Iran strikes add upside inflation risk. Warsh June 17 presser is the next major pivot point.
Household inflation expectations running hotter than forecaster models; Iran retaliating on US bases will weigh heavily on confidence and risk appetite.

bias: Maximum caution — the Iran conflict has escalated a full level. Iran directly striking US military bases in Jordan, Bahrain, and Kuwait on Jun 10 is a new threshold: this is no longer just a Strait of Hormuz chokepoint risk but a direct Iran-US military exchange on allied soil. May CPI at 4.2% in-line with core softer gives slight relief but is overwhelmed by war premium in crude. Avoid rate-sensitive longs entirely into June 17 Warsh debut. Energy (XLE, OXY), gold, and USD are the tactical hedges. Do not fade oil strength until the Strait and base-strike situation de-escalates materially.
